N3 receives at T=0 and forwards to N4 and N5 after 1 step, so N4 and N5 get N3's message at T=1.
N4 needs both messages, so it activates when the second one arrives: T=2.
N5 activates immediately on any receipt, so it first activates at T=1 from N3.

parsedOutput.final_answer:
Earliest completion time (makespan) is 6 hours. One optimal schedule is:
- Hour 0-3: Worker 1 does A; Worker 2 does B
- Hour 2-4: after B finishes, Worker 2 does D
- Hour 3-5: after A finishes, Worker 1 does C
All tasks are complete by hour 6, when D finishes.
working: A and B have no dependencies, so they can start immediately at hour 0 on the two workers.
To minimize makespan, start the two longest-independent prerequisite tasks first: A on one worker (3h) and B on the other (2h).
When B finishes at hour 2, D becomes available and can run from hour 2 to hour 6 on that worker.
When A finishes at hour 3, C becomes available and can run from hour 3 to hour 5 on the other worker.
The last task to finish is D at hour 6, so the makespan is 6 hours.
This is optimal because the total work is 11 hours on 2 workers, giving a lower bound of ceil(11/2)=6 hours, and the schedule achieves it.
